title: Glacier Sensor Net: Spare-Module Staging on a Supply Tree with Storage-Locker Caps
strategies: trivial: full-locker gold-plate, stock_i = min(4*sd_i, cap_i) (the evaluator baseline; always feasible) -> ~0.1
greedy: pure node-wise newsvendor k*=Phi^{-1}(1-h/p) clamped to the locker, ignoring the network availability floor; cheap+feasible on loose instances, violates beta on tight ones -> 0 there

strong: capacity-aware Lagrangian water-filling -- inflate every station's penalty by lambda, clamp each stock to its hard locker cap_i, and bisect lambda to the smallest value meeting the aggregate fill rate; when cheap tight lockers saturate, service is re-routed onto roomier stations (the correct KKT response to binding capacities)
invalid: hold zero spares everywhere -> aggregate availability collapses below beta on every instance -> infeasible -> 0
